title: User-type profile YAML
description: Shape of presets/profiles/<name>.yaml. Profiles overlay autopilot defaults to express user-type preferences (codex pass counts, auto-merge, PR templates, audit logging, contributor policy). Schema is exhaustive — adding a new key is a minor-version bump that requires updating this schema. Filename ↔ `profile` field matching is enforced by the resolver, not by JSON Schema (no access to external filename context).

protocol_version.description: Cadence protocol version this profile targets. Optional — defaults to '1.0.0' (the v8.5.x ecosystem baseline). Loader normalizes '1' / '1.2' to full triplet before validation. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-27-protocol-versioning-design.md.

profile.description: Profile identifier. Must match the file's basename (without `.yaml`); the resolver enforces filename ↔ field equivalence after schema validation.

description.description: Human-readable one-line summary surfaced by `profile show` / `profile list` and the init prompt.

codex_passes.description: Number of Codex review passes per risk tier. Each value is an integer in [0, 5]; 0 means skip Codex review entirely for that tier.

low.description: Codex review passes for Low-risk specs.

medium.description: Codex review passes for Medium-risk specs.

high.description: Codex review passes for High-risk specs. May be bumped per-PR by `contributor_policy.external_high_codex_passes` for external contributors.

auto_merge.description: When true (solo default), autopilot merges after all checks pass without waiting for human approval. When false, the run stops after Codex/bugbot and the PR awaits explicit merge.

require_risk_frontmatter.description: When true (enterprise default), autopilot refuses to start a spec that lacks a `risk:` frontmatter field. Forces explicit risk tier declaration.

items.description: Autopilot step number (0..9). After the listed step completes, autopilot pauses for explicit user confirmation before continuing. Used by the `learning` profile.

pause_at_steps.description: Autopilot step numbers (each 0..9, deduplicated) at which the pipeline pauses for explicit user confirmation before continuing. Empty array means no pauses (default). The `learning` profile is the only shipped profile that uses this — it explicitly carves out an exception to the otherwise-strict no-pause invariant.

audit_log_path.description: Relative DIRECTORY inside the repo (not a file path) where append-only audit logs are written as `<audit_log_path>/<run-ulid>.jsonl`. The resolver / consumer is responsible for joining the run-ulid filename — the value here names the parent directory only (e.g. `.claude/audit/`). `null` disables audit logging. Must be a POSIX-style relative path: no leading `/` (POSIX absolute), no leading drive letter `C:` (Windows absolute), no leading `\\` (UNC), no backslashes, and no `..` segments. The consumer pins the per-run filename to keep ULID monotonic ordering.

codex_explanations.description: When true (learning default), each Codex pass is followed by a second LLM pass that adds 'why this matters' prose. Additive — the raw severity / title / suggestion fields are preserved unchanged so downstream tools see the same data.

pr_template_path.description: Package-root-relative path to a PR-body Markdown template (e.g. `presets/profiles/templates/small-team-pr-template.md`). `null` falls back to the built-in default PR template. Must be a POSIX-style relative path: no leading `/` (POSIX absolute), no leading drive letter `C:` (Windows absolute), no leading `\\` (UNC), no backslashes, and no `..` segments.

contributor_policy.description: External-contributor overlay (oss-maintainer profile). When non-null, bumps the high-risk Codex pass count for PRs opened by non-org-members per the `gh api orgs/<org>/members/<user>` check.

external_high_codex_passes.description: Number of high-risk Codex passes for confirmed external contributors. Replaces (does not stack with) `codex_passes.high` for those PRs.

membership_provider.description: Which membership provider to query. Only `github-org` is supported in v8.2.0; reserved enum for future providers (e.g. `gitlab-group`).

phases.description: Per-phase provider routing override (v8.5.0+). Each routed phase (review, council, bugbot_triage) can pin its own provider + model + baseUrl. Precedence: profile > env > adapter default. The `implement` phase is intentionally absent — Claude Code's session model is not overridable from outside.

phaseRoute.description: Override block for a single phase. `provider` is required so the route is never ambiguous; `model` and `baseUrl` fall back to provider-specific defaults from the registry.

provider.description: Provider identifier — must match a built-in review-engine adapter. Providers like Groq, Ollama, DeepSeek, Together, Fireworks, Perplexity, OpenRouter, and xAI are reachable via `openai-compatible` + an explicit `baseUrl`.

model.description: Model identifier passed verbatim to the provider's API. When omitted, the provider's default for this phase is used (see PROVIDER_REGISTRY).

baseUrl.description: Optional API endpoint override. Ignored (with a warning) for providers whose SDK does not support a custom base URL (e.g. anthropic, google, bedrock, cohere).
